extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked inside final 1km of steep finish to Lagos de Covadonga, distancing LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel and VALVERDE Alejandro to reclaim red jersey
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA Nairo's repeated waiting tactics on steep gradients allowed YATES Simon to escape solo despite numerical parity
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel pulling QUINTANA rather than attacking independently reveals deference hierarchy cost both riders the stage
YATES Simon attacked decisively when gap narrowed quickly showing he recognized pursuit momentum as trigger not numeric advantage
VALVERDE Alejandro conserving for final sprint on steep finish almost caught YATES proving late acceleration viable on this gradient

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacked instinctively without hesitation on steepest section, felt more deserving of jersey than previous stint
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: passive waiting tactics on critical climb allowed inferior situation to develop despite team numbers
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: neutral: strong enough to close gaps but deferred to QUINTANA tactically rather than imposing own pace
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: nearly caught YATES in final sprint despite energy spent bridging showing exceptional finishing speed on steep gradients
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: crashed on tightening corner with stanchion contact before decisive climbing action
rider_signals.HERRADA Jesús: negative: couldn't hold position with lead group when gradient hit 15 percent
